page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.3xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance trade-off between single-threaded and multi-threaded operations. Single-core CPU performance is weak, scoring in the bottom 25% on stress-ng and Geekbench tests, with single-threaded gzip compression falling into the bottom 10%. However, the 16 dedicated physical cores deliver average multi-core performance, as evidenced by middle-tier results in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is average across both cached small-block and uncached large-block reads and writes.

The instance is suited for workloads that benefit from parallel processing rather than high single-thread throughput. It achieves average performance in static web serving, Redis SET operations, and database operations. LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but drop to weak for prompt processing on medium 7B models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation provides stable, non-burstable compute resources, making it a predictable option for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and general-purpose business applications that do not require local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with a KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct trade-off: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression are weak, while multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations score in the average tier. The dedicated physical cores ensure stable resource allocation without burstable performance degradation. This hardware profile is appropriate for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and Redis deployments that require a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio but do not depend on high single-threaded speed or local SSD storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance, suitable for parallel workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.3xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core general-purpose instance delivering balanced memory and stable multi-threaded performance for standard business workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY physical cores running at 2.5 GHz.
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core.
Average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance.
Weak single-core performance and single-threaded compression speeds.
No local storage or GPU accelerators, with 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Suited for static web serving, multi-threaded applications, and Redis.
